summary: Guan Yu; the famous single-sword banquet across the river with Lu Su. Crosses the Han River with only the green-dragon saber and one boatload of guards; eats and drinks at the banquet inside Wu territory holding Lu Su's hand the whole time so Wu's hidden archers won't risk shooting their own commander. Returns safely. The chapter's standout set piece.
Lu Su; sets the banquet trap to extort Jingzhou back; Guan Yu's hand-holding gambit neutralises it. The trap that doesn't spring.
Liu Bei; nominally agrees to return three commanderies to Wu — but Guan Yu refuses to hand them over, escalating the Jingzhou crisis.

Cao Cao; finally elevated to Duke of Wei in this chapter; the act that ends the polite fiction of Han loyalty. Then exposes Empress Fu's secret letter to her father Fu Wan plotting his death; raids the palace and strangles her with a silken cord, killing two princes by poison alongside. The grim climax of the chapter.
Dong Cheng; named in passing in the chapter's recap of the previous belt-decree.
Fu Wan; Empress Fu's father; conspires with her by secret letter; killed by Cao Cao when the plot is exposed.
Fu Gan; Empress Fu's relative; executed in the Fu purge.
Du Xi; senior official killed in the Fu purge.
Hua Xin; leads the palace raid that finds Empress Fu hidden behind a wall and drags her out by the hair — the chapter's most visceral moment of regime violence.
Lady Cao; Cao Cao's daughter; installed as the new empress after Empress Fu's murder.
Jia Xu; advises Cao Cao through the Hanzhong setup.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Xun You; in this chapter named — soon dies (named in passing of his last days).
Xun Yu; named in passing — already dead.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xu Chu; with Cao Cao in the palace raid.
Xiahou Dun; with Cao Cao.
Cao Ren; with Cao Cao.
Kan Ze; named in passing at Sun Quan's court.
Gan Ning; with Sun Quan.
Wang Can; named in passing at the Wei court.
Wei Kai; minor figure named in passing.
Wei Kai (duplicate record).
Bing Yuan; minor figure named in passing.
Guan Ning; minor figure named in passing.
Guan Ping; with Guan Yu at the river banquet.
Sun Quan; orchestrates the banquet plan with Lu Su.
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Zhuge Jin; with Sun Quan.
Zhuge Liang; at Chengdu — receives the Jingzhou diplomatic crisis updates.
Ma Liang; with Liu Bei.
Zhang Lu; off-stage at Hanzhong.

Cao Cao; the Hanzhong campaign — defeats Zhang Lu's brother Zhang Wei at Yangping Pass; then accepts Zhang Lu's surrender. Hanzhong now Wei territory. Liu Ye and Sima Yi urge an immediate push south into Yi Province but Cao Cao demurs.
Zhang Lu; surrenders Hanzhong to Cao Cao after Yangping Pass falls; treated honourably with the title Lord of Langzhong.
Zhang Wei; Zhang Lu's brother; loses Yangping Pass and is killed in the retreat.
Zhang Wei (duplicate record).
Zhang Wei (duplicate record).
Yang Ang; Zhang Wei's officer at Yangping Pass.
Yan Pu; Zhang Lu's senior counsel; advises the eventual surrender.

Pang De; defends Hanzhong under Zhang Lu — fights Cao Cao's three named champions to a draw (Xu Chu, Xu Huang, Zhang He). Eventually surrenders to Cao Cao via Jia Xu's bribery scheme; becomes a Wei general for life.
Jia Xu; designs the bribery scheme that turns Pang De.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Xu Chu; fights Pang De at Yangping.
Xu Huang; fights Pang De at Yangping.
Zhang He; fights Pang De at Yangping.
Xiahou Yuan; with Cao Cao in the Hanzhong campaign.
Xiahou Dun; with Cao Cao in the Hanzhong campaign.

Zhang Liao; in the famous parallel sub-plot — defends Hefei against Sun Quan's 100,000-strong attack with only 7,000 men. The chapter's standout passage is the night-attack at Xiaoyao Ford where Zhang Liao with 800 picked men charges Sun Quan's whole camp at dawn, sending Sun Quan up a hill in panic. The defining 'Children stop crying at the sound of Zhang Liao' chapter.
Li Dian; defends Hefei with Zhang Liao.
Sun Quan; nearly killed at Xiaoyao Ford by Zhang Liao's charge; pulled to safety only by Ling Tong, Gu Li, and a bridge that breaks under his horse before being heaved across by his own steed's leap.
Gu Li; Sun Quan officer at Hefei.
Gu Li (duplicate record).
Gan Ning; pulls Sun Quan out of the Xiaoyao Ford rout.
Cheng Pu; with Sun Quan.
Han Dang; with Sun Quan.
Jiang Qin; with Sun Quan.
Chen Wu; killed at Xiaoyao Ford.
Dong Xi; killed at Xiaoyao Ford.
Pan Zhang; with Sun Quan.
Huang Gai; with Sun Quan.
Huang Gai (duplicate record).
Xu Sheng; with Sun Quan.
Zhou Tai; covers Sun Quan's escape at Xiaoyao Ford with his body — takes the 14 wound scars later listed at the post-campaign banquet.
Zhou Tai (duplicate record).
Zhu Guang; Lujiang officer killed by Sun Quan's army earlier in the chapter.
Lu Su; with Sun Quan.
Xue Ti; envoy named in passing.
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Liu Ye; advises Cao Cao to push immediately into Yi Province after Hanzhong falls — ignored.
Sima Yi; backs Liu Ye's advice to push into Yi Province now; Cao Cao refuses the gamble.
Liu Zhang; long-deposed; named in passing.
Liu Bei; at Chengdu; alarmed by Cao Cao's Hanzhong victory.
Guan Yu; at Jingzhou.
Zhang Fei; at Langzhong.
Liang Da; minor figure named in passing.
Ma Chao; with Liu Bei at Chengdu.
Cao Ren; with Cao Cao.
Yi Ji; with Liu Bei.
Zhuge Jin; sent to Liu Bei to demand Jingzhou's return — Liu Bei agrees in principle and forwards to Guan Yu, who refuses.
Zhuge Liang; at Chengdu.

Gan Ning; the famous 100-horseman night raid into Cao Cao's main camp at Ruxu. Hand-picks 100 men, ties white wild-goose feathers to their helmets, breaks into the Cao camp at dawn with drums beating, kills dozens, and returns without losing a single rider. The chapter's signature raid.
Sun Quan; rewards Gan Ning lavishly after the raid; 'with such a man, I do not fear Cao Cao'. Then sets up the parallel Hefei front.

Cao Cao; at Ruxu again; suffers Gan Ning's raid; receives the Daoist Zuo Ci who performs uncanny tricks at his court banquet (the pomegranate-turns-to-water, the wine-from-the-air, the head-still-talking decapitation). Cao Cao orders his execution; Zuo Ci vanishes through a wall.

Zuo Ci; the Daoist 'Black Horn' immortal; chapter set piece. Demonstrates a series of unsettling magical tricks at Cao Cao's banquet — pulling fish from a hole, growing peonies in the dish, walking through walls — then disappears with the line 'you have done much evil, mend your ways'.
Cao Pi; at Cao Cao's court.
Cao Zhi; at Cao Cao's court; the famous 'Seven Steps' poem competition with his brother is set up in this chapter.
Cao Zhang; Cao Cao's third son; in the chapter's family roll.
Cao Xiu; in Cao Cao's family roll.
Cao Xiong; in Cao Cao's family roll.
Cao Ang; named in passing in the family recap.
Lady Bian; Cao Cao's principal wife; mother of Cao Pi, Cao Zhi, Cao Zhang. Named in the family scene.
Jia Xu; advises Cao Cao.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Xu Chu; Cao Cao's bodyguard.
Xu Huang; with Cao Cao at Ruxu.
Cao Ren; with Cao Cao at Ruxu.
Li Dian; with Cao Cao at Ruxu.
Zhang Liao; with Cao Cao at Ruxu after Hefei.
Pang De; now serving Cao Cao after the Hanzhong transition.
Zhong Yao; senior Wei minister.
Cui Yan; senior Wei minister.
Xun Yu; named in passing — long dead.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Jiang Qin; with Sun Quan.
Chen Wu; named in passing — dies at Hefei.
Dong Xi; named in passing — dies at Hefei.
Zhou Tai; honoured by Sun Quan at a great banquet with the count of his 14 wound scars.
Zhou Tai (duplicate record).
Lu Xun; with Sun Quan.
Xu Sheng; with Sun Quan.
Liang Da; minor figure named in passing.
Lady Liu; named in passing.
Lady Liu (duplicate record).
Liu Biao; named in passing.
Sun Ce; named in passing.
Yuan Shao; named in passing.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu; named in passing — already dead.
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Xiu (duplicate record).
Zhang Zhao; with Sun Quan.
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Gu Yong; with Sun Quan.
Bu Zhi; with Sun Quan.
Liu Bei; off-stage at Chengdu.
Guan Lu; the famous diviner; reads the Book of Changes to predict Cao Cao's coming illnesses, the death of Xun Yu, the rebellion of the loyalists, and many other events. The chapter's centrepiece.

Geng Ji; loyalist conspirator; with four others plans to assassinate Cao Cao at the New Year court. The plot leaks; all five and their families are executed. The novel's last major Han-loyalist conspiracy.
Wei Huang; co-conspirator with Geng Ji; executed in the purge.
Jin Yi; co-conspirator with Geng Ji; executed.
Ji Mao; co-conspirator; executed.
Ji Mu; co-conspirator; executed.
Cao Cao; ill through the chapter; murders the five loyalist conspirators when their plot is exposed; sends Cao Hong and Cao Xiu against Liu Bei's frontier raids.
Cao Hong; sent against Liu Bei's Hanzhong-frontier campaign — defeats Wu Lan.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Cao Xiu; sent with Cao Hong against Liu Bei's frontier raids.
Ren Kui; killed in Cao Hong's raid.
Wu Lan; Liu Bei officer; killed in Cao Hong's raid.
Liu Bei; loses Wu Lan on the western frontier; begins the long planning toward the Hanzhong campaign.
Lei Tong; minor figure named in passing.
Ma Chao; in this chapter named — operates on the western frontier alongside Wu Lan.
Zhang Fei; in this chapter named — at Langzhong.
Dong Cheng; named in passing in the conspiracy recap.
Hua Xin; at Cao Cao's court.
Lu Su; named in passing — long dying year approaching.
Liu Bin; minor figure named in passing.
Sima Yi; at Cao Cao's court.
Guo En; minor figure named in passing.
Shan Zichun; named in the chapter's biographical recap of Guan Lu.
Wang Bi; loyal Wei officer in charge of the New Year ceremonies; killed by the conspirators in their attack.
Wang Bi (duplicate record).
Wang Bi (duplicate record).
Wang Ji; minor figure named in passing.
Wang Ji (duplicate record).
Wang Ji (duplicate record).
Wang Ji (duplicate record).
Xiahou Dun; with Cao Cao.
Xiahou Yuan; sent to defend Hanzhong; will be killed at Dingjun in chapter 71.
Xu Zhi; minor figure named in passing.
Xu Zhi (duplicate record).
Xu Zhi (duplicate record).
Zhang He; sent to Hanzhong with Xiahou Yuan.
Zhong Yao; senior minister at Cao Cao's court.
Zhuge Yuan; minor figure named in passing.

Zhang Fei; the famous Wakou Pass campaign — wins against Zhang He by pretending to drink himself into a stupor every day in front of the pass. Zhang He attacks the drunken-looking camp; the camp turns out to be a straw-dummy ambush; Zhang He loses three camps and the pass. The pivot that begins the Hanzhong reconquest.
Zhang He; defeated by Zhang Fei's drunkard trick at Wakou Pass; loses three camps and retreats to Mt Wakou. Defeated again later by Wei Yan and Lei Tong's attack.

Huang Zhong; the Tiandang Mountain campaign — defeats Xiahou Shang and Han Hao, takes their grain depot and the mountain in a series of single combats. The chapter's establishing showcase for his age-defying skill.
Xiahou Shang; killed by Huang Zhong at Tiandang Mountain.
Han Hao; killed by Huang Zhong at Tiandang Mountain.
Xiahou Yuan; loses Tiandang; called by Cao Cao to fall back on Dingjun Mountain.
Liu Bei; sets up the Hanzhong reconquest — sends Zhang Fei against Zhang He, Huang Zhong against Xiahou Yuan.
Zhuge Liang; designs the Hanzhong campaign; deliberately goads Huang Zhong with age-jabs to ensure his energy. The 'goad the old general' technique that he uses again at chapter 71.
Fa Zheng; appointed Huang Zhong's strategist in this chapter — will be the architect of the Dingjun ambush in chapter 71.
Liu Feng; commands part of the Hanzhong assault.
Meng Da; commands part of the Hanzhong assault.
Meng Da (duplicate record).
Wei Yan; partners Lei Tong in the Wakou Pass fight.
Wei Yan (duplicate record).
Lei Tong; partners Wei Yan in the Wakou Pass fight; killed in the chapter.
Guan Yu; named in passing at Jingzhou.
Zhao Yun; with Liu Bei at Chengdu.
Ma Chao; with Liu Bei.
Huo Jun; named in passing.
Jiang Ji; named in passing.
Cao Cao; receives the news of Hanzhong losses; orders Xiahou Yuan to hold Dingjun Mountain at all costs. Will march in person to relieve.
Cao Hong; named in passing.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Xiahou Dun; at Cao Cao's court.
Han Xuan; named in passing — old Changsha context.
Han Xuan (duplicate record).
Guo Huai; Xiahou Yuan's adviser at Dingjun Mountain.
Guo Huai (duplicate record).
Guo Huai (duplicate record).
Gu Ti; minor figure named in passing.
Sun Chen; minor figure named in passing.
Yan Yan; sent against Cao Hong; ages-old veteran commander.
Yan Yan (duplicate record).
Zhang Lu; named in passing — long surrendered.
Shentu Pan; minor figure named in passing.
